svg.text: Request 4: PEP hand-off feasibility
Bounded acquisition/build gate for the external weak-field estimator hand-off.
Decision
verdict = PEP is confirmed as the right external estimator family, but it is not locally buildable on the current arm64 gfortran toolchain.
current machine = arm64 | GNU Fortran (Homebrew GCC 14.2.0_1) 14.2.0
REAL*10 files / hits = 166 / 207
long-double flag sites = ['verify/makefile', 'peputil/makefile']
next bounded action = try only a separate x86_64 / legacy-compatible build environment; do not turn Request 4 into a local PEP porting project
Diagnostics
PASS · public acquisition
Cloned pep_core@570410b and pep_doc@146d799 from public GitLab remotes.
PASS · LLR pedigree present
bigtest.obsllr exists=True and peputil/bigtest workflow references it directly=True.
FAIL · arm64 REAL*10 support
Current gfortran rejects both REAL*10 declarations and _10 literals in a trivial probe.
FAIL · arm64 long-double flag
Current gfortran rejects -m128bit-long-double, while pep_core/verify and peputil makefiles require that flag.
FAIL · minimal object build
make -C pep -f makefile a1ut1.o fails immediately at the first REAL*10 source, before any LLR bigtest run can start.
WARN · x86 legacy toolchain on host
Rosetta available=True, but dedicated /usr/local/bin gfortran paths found=0.
WARN · porting risk
REAL*10 appears in 166 files and 207 source hits, so rewriting kinds locally would become a porting project, not a bounded hand-off.
